macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Bank of Canada Holds Rate Steady at 2.25% Amid Iran War Uncertainty
items.summary: The Bank of Canada maintained its policy rate at 2.25% on March 18, 2026, as the central bank navigates conflicting economic signals from the Middle East conflict. Policymakers are relying heavily on judgment to balance rising inflation from energy prices against Canada's economic underperformance.

items.title: Global Stock Markets Face Volatility as Iran Conflict Disrupts Oil Supply
items.summary: The war in Iran has shut down a large fraction of the world's oil, liquefied natural gas, and fertilizer shipments, with the Strait of Hormuz closure cutting off 20% of global oil supply. Brent crude has surged 50% since military strikes began on February 27th, creating intense market volatility.

items.title: Energy Shock Drives Inflation Concerns Across Central Banks
items.summary: Rising energy prices from the Iran conflict are forcing central banks to rethink rate cut expectations, with the Federal Reserve likely postponing further cuts until at least 2027. U.S. Treasury yields have jumped nearly 50 basis points since the war began, threatening to increase corporate borrowing costs.
items.source: RBC Wealth Management / Bank of Canada

items.title: U.S. Economy Shows Resilience Despite War-Related Headwinds
items.summary: The U.S. labor market remains stable with low unemployment claims, and manufacturing activity is showing broad-based improvement. However, RBC analysts are not forecasting a recession but warn that each month the war continues will chip away at full-year GDP growth.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips 3% to $67,000 Amid Trump's Iran Escalation Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped nearly 3% in the past 24 hours to around $67,000 following President Trump's signals of continued US military action in Iran, impacting market sentiment. Major cryptocurrencies lost ground due to lack of conciliatory remarks and increased global volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Slumps to $66,000 After Trump's National Address Reversal
items.summary: Bitcoin fell 2.91% to approximately $66,000 as cryptocurrencies declined following President Trump's shift in tone during his national address on Iran tensions. The market experienced risk aversion and volatility.

items.title: DRIFT Token Crashes Over 36% in 24 Hours to $0.043
items.summary: The DRIFT token plummeted more than 36% over the past 24 hours, currently trading at $0.043 according to HTX market data. This marks a significant price movement in the cryptocurrency.

items.title: Expert Identifies Bitcoin Prime Buy Zone Below $60,000
items.summary: Analysis suggests Bitcoin has potential to fall further this year, creating a prime buying opportunity below $60,000 based on historical price patterns. This sparks optimism for long-term investors.

items.title: Cardano Poised for Major Price Surge with Bullish Indicators
items.summary: Cardano is positioned for an explosive upward move, supported by a large price base and bullish MACD divergence amid broader market optimism. Analysts anticipate a significant surge.

items.title: XRP Faces Further Declines with Spot-Derivatives Divergence
items.summary: XRP shows significant divergence between spot and derivatives markets, with analysts forecasting more pain in Q2 and potential deeper pullbacks. Ethereum also flashes warning signals.

items.title: Rising Interest in Quantum-Resistant Cryptocurrencies Like Zcash
items.summary: Google's quantum computing advancements have boosted interest in quantum-resistant cryptocurrencies such as Zcash amid growing security concerns in the blockchain space.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Faces Criticism from Larian Studios Over Recent Update
items.summary: Larian Studios, creators of Baldur's Gate 3, called out Crimson Desert following a strange news update from Pearl Abyss. The game continues to generate buzz with ongoing developments and impressive sales figures.

items.title: Top 10 New Games Announced for April 2026 Release
items.summary: Upcoming releases include Road to Vostok (April 7), Lay of the Land (April 8), Last Flag (April 14), Pragmata (April 17), and Masters of Albion (April 22), alongside updates for Starfield, Soulmask, and Diablo 4.

items.title: RAID Shadow Legends Releases Official Patch Notes 11.40.0
items.summary: The latest patch introduces subscription changes, numerous bug fixes, and buffs to champions, with one now considered a meta pick in arena and PvE. Includes details on relic crafting bugs and account giveaways.

items.title: Fighting Game News: New Trailers for Akali in 2XKO, Azula in Avatar Legends, Conquest in Invincible
items.summary: Recent trailers showcase Akali for 2XKO, Azula and others for Avatar Legends (releasing July 2nd for $30), and Conquest DLC for Invincible, highlighting upcoming fighters and roster expansions.

items.title: Kena: Bridge of Spirits Port to Nintendo Switch 2 Receives Mixed Reviews
items.summary: The newly released Switch 2 port targets 30 FPS, falling short of 60 FPS expectations, with critics noting performance issues despite available tools for better optimization.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: IRGC Declares Apple, Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Tesla as Legitimate Targets
items.summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has named major U.S. tech giants including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Tesla as legitimate targets amid escalating Middle East tensions. Employees have been advised to evacuate offices in the region due to the serious threat.

items.title: Iranian Strike Damages Amazon AWS Data Center in Bahrain
items.summary: A reported Iranian attack has hit an Amazon Web Services (AWS) facility in Bahrain, causing damage to critical cloud infrastructure supporting businesses and governments across West Asia. The extent of operational impact remains unclear, with no official confirmation from AWS.
